summary: The iteration-3 design (design.md) and specs (status_summary_test.rb, plus the referenced broadcast_status/manager_tick/step_run and pipeline_live_status specs) are strongly aligned with both guides, and the two minor findings from the prior iteration are now resolved or properly justified. Backend-guide: derivation logic lives in a reusable, side-effect-free domain PORO at app/lib/pipelines/status_summary.rb with a read-only `.for` interface — correctly NOT the `.call` the guide reserves for verb-first services that mutate (backend-guide 'Services — Uniform interface' / 'app/lib for app-specific POROs that aren't services/queries'); the summary broadcast is a thin service (Pipelines::BroadcastStatus) that faithfully mirrors the existing StepRuns::BroadcastCard, firing after commit from the run/phase transition seams, targeting the smallest DOM unit keyed by dom_id (backend-guide 'Broadcasts happen from services, after commit; target the smallest partial'); controllers stay thin with a persistence-only `with_board` scope on the model (backend-guide 'Controllers — light' / 'Models: associations, scopes'); no business logic in callbacks or jobs; tests target the PORO and services as the primary unit (backend-guide 'Testing'). UI-guide: the _status_summary partial is one source of truth (full + compact via a `compact:` local) so the two surfaces can never disagree; it uses the Card classes, the defined type scale (text-sm / text-xs uppercase eyebrow), Tailwind spacing steps, aria-live='polite' live region, and a semantic status dot ALWAYS paired with the status word so meaning is never color-alone (ui-style-guide 'Color — semantic and reserved', 'Core components — one source of truth', 'Accessibility — status never conveyed by color alone', 'Real-time — smallest DOM unit / true state on load'). Verified against the live repo: the pipeline status enum (draft/running/awaiting_human/blocked/stuck/completed/aborted), phase `failed` status, StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES/TONE_CLASSES, and StepRuns::BroadcastCard all exist as cited. Prior F1 (an aborted pipeline would show a gray summary dot but a red status_badge, violating 'semantic colors reserved' + 'one source of truth per component') is now fully reconciled: every branch sources its tone from the shared STATUS_TONES table, the one real conflict is fixed at that single source by retoning 'aborted' :danger->:muted, and the matching ui-style-guide color-table row is proposed in the same PR per CLAUDE.md — the specs now assert the summary tone equals STATUS_TONES.fetch(status) and that aborted's summary tone equals its badge tone. Prior F2 (backend-guide names system tests for the board flow, but the flow is covered by an ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest request spec) remains a deviation from the guide's letter, but it is forced by a repo-wide gap — there is no system-test base class or Capybara setup — and the design handles it exactly as the guides/CLAUDE.md prescribe: it calls the substitution out explicitly, notes the request spec covers everything short of the in-browser in-place repaint, and records adding an ApplicationSystemTestCase + browser test as a Build follow-up. That is a justified, documented pragmatic substitution rather than an unaddressed guide violation. No blocker, major, or minor guide deviations remain unresolved. (Non-guide nit for Build, not a finding: design §3.4 states app/helpers/pipelines_helper.rb 'file exists, empty' and lists it under Edited, but that file does not exist in the repo — it is a new file.)
